Entry.JapaneseText: どうやら 君を更生させる事は僕には無理らしい
Entry.EnglishText: So in the end, it looks like I won't be able to get you to start anew.

Entry.JapaneseText: そういうこった。 何事も諦めが肝心ってな
Entry.EnglishText: That's how it is. Giving up is the most essential part.

Entry.JapaneseText: とりあえず 今度誰かを説得しに行くときは 君を連れていかないようにするよ
Entry.EnglishText: In any case, next time I need to persuade someone, I won't bring you along.

Entry.JapaneseText: そりゃ嬉しいね。 せいせいするぜ
Entry.EnglishText: Happy to hear it. That's a relief.

Entry.JapaneseText: だがまぁ、 なにかあったときはいつでも言えよ
Entry.EnglishText: But, well, if anything happens, you can tell me anytime.

Entry.JapaneseText: 違う道を歩いてるからこそ 出来る事もあるはずだから、な
Entry.EnglishText: Since we walk different paths, there's gotta be stuff we can both do, right?
Entry.Notes: More literally, it's just like, "there's stuff that can be done". I believe the implication here is their usual, we walk different paths, but there are still things both of us can do from our own end of things.
